title: Tether Confirm Button
prompt:
Create a production-ready Tether Confirm Button using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original control confirms a compact handoff: a small lime tether connects a left status pin to a right confirmation dot when the action is active. The label changes from Confirm handoff to Handoff confirmed, while a plain language status sentence remains below. Place it inside a light Bento composition with a #F4F5F6 stage, white surface, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, a very soft neutral shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Lime is the only bright accent, and neither card nor control may use a gradient.

Use one native button with a 44px minimum target, aria-pressed, an explicit accessible name, and an aria-live polite status. Click, Enter, and Space must safely toggle the state. Animate the tether width and confirmation dot with a spring using stiffness 340 and damping 26; use a 150ms ease-out opacity fade for the label, and a 90ms press scale of 0.98. Keep all data local and deterministic: no timers, random values, storage, remote assets, network requests, or layout-measuring code. Prefer transform and opacity for motion.

At 320px, preserve the label and pin shapes without clipping; on a large detail preview, permit a comfortable maximum width. Include a visible 3px focus ring, logical tab order, AA contrast, and both text and shape cues. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the tether and retaining only a brief opacity change. Return complete paste-ready client code plus a runnable pure CSS approximation and suggested HTML.
demoCode:
(() => {function Demo(){const [on,setOn]=React.useState(false);return
HANDOFF

{on?'The handoff is confirmed.':'The handoff still needs confirmation.'}

}return ;})()
